frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: current responses biased by previous trial features

frameworks.name: short-term memory trace
frameworks.one_line: intertrial neural trace mediates the across-trial bias

frameworks.name: causal TMS perturbation
frameworks.one_line: TMS-injected noise degrades putative memory traces

core_question: Which cortical region carries the across-trial neural trace that produces serial dependence in a visuomotor interception task?
key_assumptions: previous-trial information leaves a neural trace in task-relevant regions
TMS bursts inject noise that degrades local memory traces
interception speed bias indexes serial dependence
manipulations: TMS site: PMd vs hV5/MT+ vs Vertex control
TMS bursts applied during intertrial intervals
coincident timing task with varied target speed
key_findings: without TMS, interception was biased toward previous trial's target speed
TMS over left dorsal premotor cortex reduced serial dependence
TMS over right hV5/MT+ did not reduce serial dependence
interpretations: premotor cortex hosts an implicit short-term memory mechanism that carries information across trials
visual motion region hV5/MT+ is not the locus of this across-trial trace
